description: A person or group becomes a 'wemi' by committing practical support to another person, group, or cause through a shared struggle or undertaking. The relation is chosen and need not be friendship. 'tewema' (partner) centres a pair, while allyship can join many participants around one course.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then move into stressed middle-height front unrounded 'e'. Close the lips for voiced 'm', carry the sound through the nose, and release into high-front unrounded 'i'.
sound_symbolism: The opening 'we' is also the stressed centre of 'tewema' (partner). Here it begins the shorter word and closes quickly at the lips. The neighbouring relation has a more compact sound.
usage_notes: Possessor-first 'mia wemi' means my ally. The surrounding account names the cause and the acts of support, which are what allow an alliance to be judged.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Collective action needs words for people who take another group's struggle into their own practical commitments. 'wemi' names that relation while the work itself stays visible.

semantic_domains.activity: practical support committed within a shared undertaking
semantic_domains.community: a chosen relation of solidarity with a person, group, or cause
